questions.text: A set is a well-defined collection of:
questions.explanation: By definition, a set is a well-defined collection of distinct objects.

questions.text: The set of all vowels in the English alphabet is represented in roster form as:
questions.explanation: Roster form lists all elements separated by commas within braces.

questions.text: A set containing no elements is called a/an:
questions.explanation: An empty set (or null set/void set) is denoted by φ or {}.

questions.text: If A = {1, 2} and B = {1, 2, 3}, then:
options: A is a subset of B
B is a subset of A
questions.explanation: Every element of A is also an element of B, so A ⊂ B.

questions.text: The number of subsets of a set with 'n' elements is:
questions.explanation: The power set of a set with n elements has 2^n elements.

questions.text: The intersection of two disjoint sets is:
questions.explanation: Disjoint sets have no common elements, so their intersection is φ.

questions.text: If A = {1, 2, 3} and B = {3, 4, 5}, then A ∪ B is:
questions.explanation: Union combines all elements, but duplicates are written only once.

questions.text: The complement of the universal set is:
questions.explanation: U' = U - U = φ.

questions.text: A set with only one element is called a:
questions.explanation: Example: {5} is a singleton set.

questions.text: In set-builder notation, A = {x : x is an even natural number < 10} is:
questions.explanation: Natural numbers start from 1. Even numbers less than 10 are 2, 4, 6, 8.

questions.text: The set of real numbers is denoted by:
questions.explanation: R stands for Real numbers, Q for Rationals, Z for Integers, N for Naturals.

questions.text: If A ⊂ B, then A ∪ B equals:
questions.explanation: If A is contained in B, their union is simply the larger set B.

questions.text: If A ⊂ B, then A ∩ B equals:
questions.explanation: If A is contained in B, their common elements are exactly the elements of A.

questions.text: The difference A - B consists of elements that are in:
questions.explanation: A - B contains elements belonging to A that do not belong to B.

questions.text: Which of the following is an infinite set?
options: Set of days in a week
Set of points on a line
questions.explanation: A line segment contains infinitely many points.

questions.text: According to De Morgan's Law, (A ∪ B)' is equal to:
questions.explanation: The complement of the union is the intersection of the complements.

questions.text: What is the cardinality of the set A = {x : x is a letter in the word 'MATHEMATICS'}?
questions.explanation: Unique letters: M, A, T, H, E, I, C, S. Count is 8.

questions.text: If n(A) = 20, n(B) = 30 and n(A ∪ B) = 40, then n(A ∩ B) is:
The power set of an empty set has ________ elements.
questions.explanation: P(φ) = {φ}. It contains one element.

questions.text: Venn diagrams were named after:
questions.explanation: John Venn popularized these diagrams to represent set relationships.

questions.text: In a Venn diagram, the universal set is usually represented by a:
questions.explanation: The rectangle represents the universal set, and circles represent its subsets.

questions.text: The symmetric difference of A and B is (A - B) ∪ (B - A). This represents elements in:
options: Either A or B but not both
questions.explanation: It excludes the intersection of the two sets.

questions.text: Two sets A and B are equal if:
questions.explanation: This is the formal definition of set equality.

questions.text: If A = {φ, {φ}}, then the number of elements in A is:
questions.explanation: The elements are 'φ' and the set '{φ}'.

questions.text: The set {x : x ∈ R, a < x < b} is called a/an:
questions.explanation: Open intervals exclude the endpoints and are denoted by (a, b).

questions.text: The set {x : x ∈ R, a ≤ x ≤ b} is denoted by:
questions.explanation: Brackets [] indicate that the endpoints are included (closed interval).

questions.text: If A is a set with 3 elements, how many elements are in P(P(A))? (Note: P is Power Set)
For any set A, (A')' is:
questions.explanation: The complement of a complement is the original set itself.

questions.text: A ∩ (B ∪ C) = (A ∩ B) ∪ (A ∩ C) is the ________ property.
questions.explanation: Intersection distributes over union.

questions.text: The set of all irrational numbers is denoted by:
questions.explanation: Irrational numbers are real numbers that are not rational.

questions.text: If n(A - B) = 10, n(B - A) = 15 and n(A ∩ B) = 5, then n(A ∪ B) is:
Two finite sets have m and n elements. The total number of subsets of the first is 56 more than the second. The values of m and n are:
Which of the following is true for any two sets A and B?
questions.explanation: The difference A - B is equivalent to the intersection of A with the complement of B.

questions.text: The set {x : x² = 9 and 2x = 4} is:
questions.explanation: x²=9 gives x=±3. 2x=4 gives x=2. No value satisfies both.

questions.text: If A is the set of even numbers and B is the set of odd numbers, then A ∩ B is:
questions.explanation: No number can be both even and odd.

questions.text: The number of elements in the power set of {1, 2, 3, 4} is:
A ∪ A' equals:
questions.explanation: The union of a set and its complement covers the entire universal set.

questions.text: A ∩ A' equals:
questions.explanation: A set and its complement have no common elements.

questions.text: In a group of 70 people, 37 like coffee, 52 like tea. If each person likes at least one drink, how many like both?
Who is the founder of Set Theory?
questions.explanation: German mathematician Georg Cantor developed the theory of sets.
